summary: Epic The Other One marathon with soaring jams and tight set flow
blurb: The Olympia show delivers a monster second‑set suite built around The Other One, with China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segueing into relentless improvisation. Jerry’s tone is on fire, Phil lays down thunderous bass, and the band stays locked in from start to finish.
review: May 3, 1972 at the Olympia Theater captured one of the Grateful Dead’s most adventurous evenings of the early‑’70s. The first set rolls through classics like Bertha, Sugaree and a crisp China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider that sets up the high‑octane second half. From there the band launches into an extended Truckin' → The Other One → Drums → The Other One → Me & Bobby McGee → Wharf Rat marathon that showcases Jerry’s blistering solos on Hurts Me Too and The Other One, Phil’s bass thunder and jazzy excursions, and Bill’s tight, taste‑filled drumming. Highlights such as the soaring “Hurts Me Too,” the seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition, and the rapturous “Sing Me Back Home” ballad demonstrate why this show is often called a hidden gem of Europe ’72. The recording quality on the Masse SBD (gd72-05-03.sbd.masse.142.sbeok.shnf) is pristine, preserving every nuance of the band’s chemistry while keeping the audience ambience intimate.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across all five recordings: every analysis uses praise like 'pristine', 'excellent', 'stellar' and highlights the same standout jams (The Other One, China>Rider, Hurts Me Too). No negative comments about performance appear, so a high positive adjustment is warranted.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with excellent separation and only a brief dropout, preserving the full energy of the marathon jams.

key_highlights: Marathon The Other One suite that defines the second set
Seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition
Blistering Hurts Me Too solo and soaring Jerry tone
Tight rhythm section driving high‑energy improvisation

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring lead tone, blistering solos on Hurts Me Too and The Other One; wah‑wah perfect on several cuts, wild fills lift Not Fade Away and double‑bends on One More Saturday Night.
band_performance.Phil: Powerful bass bombs, jazzy solo in the second‑set jam, rumble on The Other One, completely on fire during He's Gone and tight interplay with the rest of the band.
band_performance.Bob: Solid rhythm work throughout; melodic chord moments especially in the Other One transitions, hoarse rasp that adds grit to Not Fade Away, strong vocal delivery on Sugar Magnolia.
band_performance.Brent: Keith provides sweet low‑background texture on Sing Me Back Home and goes full‑throttle in duets with Jerry during jams; stays "ON" throughout He's Gone, adding depth to the improvisations.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann delivers tight, tasteful drumming with crisp tom rolls and dynamic breaks that perfectly support Phil’s bass excursions and the band’s expansive jams.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
